role: Spiritual leader of the Front of Islamic Revolution Stability; co-founder of Haghani Seminary
bio: Mohammad-Taqi Mesbah Yazdi was the most influential ideologue of the Iranian ultra-right, described as 'a hardliner to terrify hardliners'. A student of [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) at the Qom seminary, he co-founded the [Haghani Seminary](/orgs/haghani-seminary) — the institution that trained the backbone of the Islamic Republic's clerical management class. He was the spiritual mentor of [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad) and the guiding force behind the [Front of Islamic Revolution Stability](/orgs/front-of-islamic-revolution-stability), formally established in 2011 by his students. He endorsed Saeed Jalili in the 2013 presidential election and backed [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi) in 2017. He repeatedly urged the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) to suppress the reform movement by any means. He died on 1 January 2021.
editorial_notes: Born 31 January 1935. Died 1 January 2021. Birth name Taqi Givechi. Co-founded Haghani Seminary with Mohammad Beheshti and Ahmad Jannati.
